doc.content: Anthropic is in early-stage talks with Samsung Electronics to explore manufacturing a custom AI chip, reportedly eyeing Samsung's 2nm process and advanced packaging. Verbatim: 'Anthropic was in contact with Samsung to explore a collaboration around the pending chip.' 'Anthropic hasn't yet decided what the chip will be used for, how it will fit into the server, or how powerful it will be.' On hedging vs. merchant GPUs: 'A diversified hardware stack that includes chips from Google, Amazon, and Nvidia will continue to be pivotal to its compute strategy.' Context line: 'A number of AI companies have sought to develop custom chips - both as a way to create unique hardware for specific compute tasks and to gain a certain amount of independence from Nvidia.' Article also notes this follows OpenAI's partnership with Broadcom on a custom inference processor called 'Jalapeno,' said to show 'better performance-per-watt, than other competitor chips.'
